title: Hosted and Self-Hosted Runners, Isolation, Capacity, and Compatibility · Automation Case: Ci Ephemeral Runner Cleanup
summary: A privileged self-hosted runner is reused for public pull requests, carries cloud credentials and Docker authority, drifts from its image contract, and queues releases behind unbounded jobs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci ephemeral runner cleanup increment in runner isolation and capacity policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design single-job ephemeral runner registration, identity, network segmentation, secret delivery, teardown, log retention, and orphan cleanup.
Threat-model persistence, workspace residue, credentials, network reachability, daemon sockets, labels, groups, and repository eligibility for self-hosted runners.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example public pull request reaches a persistent self-hosted runner with Docker authority and cloud credentials while 32 jobs queue. Bound eligibility, ephemerality, compatibility, capacity, evidence, and destruction. Competency probe: Design single-job ephemeral runner registration, identity, network segmentation, secret delivery, teardown, log retention, and orphan cleanup. A defensible solution applies Ci Ephemeral Runner Cleanup, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to runner isolation and capacity policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-ephemeral-runner-cleanup — Design single-job ephemeral runner registration, identity, network segmentation, secret delivery, teardown, log retention, and orphan cleanup.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-runners-isolation-capacity-5
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-runners-isolation-capacity-5
    artifact: runner-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha runners isolation capacity 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      runner-image: ubuntu-24.04-reviewed
      action-runtime: node24
      minimum-runner: 2.327.1-or-later
      untrusted-work: github-hosted-ephemeral
      self-hosted: trusted-release-only
      network: segmented
      cleanup: single-job-destroy
      capacity: queue-budget-and-max-parallel
    verify: inspect runner version image release labels groups network authority queue duration and teardown evidence
    failure: Reject latest as immutable, reused public-PR runners, workspace cleanup as host destruction, or unbounded parallelism.
    repair: Record compatibility, isolate untrusted work, destroy ephemeral capacity, segment authority, and measure queue and cost.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Deleting a runner registration guarantees its host and credentials were destroyed. This fails the Ci Ephemeral Runner Cleanup contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
